title: Bitfarms Reports Third Quarter 2024 Results
text: Bitfarms reported Q3 2024 revenue of $45 million, up 8% quarter-over-quarter and 30% year-over-year. Profitability metrics deteriorated with net loss of $37 million or ($0.08) per share. Gross mining margin compressed significantly to 38% in Q3 2024, down from 51% in Q2 2024 and 44% in prior year quarter, reflecting challenging industry conditions. Current hashrate was 11.9 EH/s, up from 10.4 EH/s in Q2. Daily production averaged 7.6 BTC per day in Q3. Energy efficiency achieved 21 w/TH, representing 16% improvement from June 30, 2024. BTC earned totaled 703 BTC at average direct cost of $36,000 per coin. Management expects to reach 21 EH/s in first half of 2025 and projects over 950 MW of energy capacity by year-end 2025 following Stronghold Digital Mining acquisition, with potential for 1.6 GW long-term expansion. CFO Jeff Lucas cited record low hashprices, 62% year-over-year increase in network difficulty, and effects from April Bitcoin halving event as Q3 challenges.
